text.zh: 最大不确定性是末期叶细胞的存活比例。
text.en: The main uncertainty is the fraction of viable leaf cells at the endpoint.

readouts.zh: 定时测量RD29A转录本或启动子报告信号。
readouts.en: Measure RD29A transcripts or promoter-reporter signal at matched sampling times.

readouts.zh: 同步测量叶片水势、相对含水量和ABA。
readouts.en: Measure leaf water potential, relative water content, and ABA concurrently.

readouts.zh: 记录气孔导度、叶温和净光合速率。
readouts.en: Record stomatal conductance, leaf temperature, and net photosynthetic rate.

readouts.zh: 检测渗透调节物、膜泄漏和叶绿素荧光。
readouts.en: Assay osmolytes, membrane leakage, and chlorophyll fluorescence.

controls.zh: 设置同基因型、同叶龄和同期取样的充足供水对照。
controls.en: Use well-watered controls matched for genotype, leaf age, and sampling time.

controls.zh: 匹配光周期、温湿度、营养和土壤体积。
controls.en: Match photoperiod, temperature, humidity, nutrition, and soil volume.

controls.zh: 用空报告载体或未诱导组织控制背景信号。
controls.en: Use empty-reporter or unstressed tissue controls for background signal.

title.zh: 复水后RD29A关联叶片状态轨迹

geneFunction.zh: RD29A表达可指示脱水诱导的ABA依赖与非依赖应激程序。
geneFunction.en: RD29A expression reports dehydration-induced ABA-dependent and ABA-independent stress programs.

state.zh: RD29A关联的早期脱水转录状态开始增强。
state.en: An early dehydration transcriptional state associated with RD29A begins strengthening.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞和叶肉细胞启动ABA与渗透应答。
cell.en: Guard and mesophyll cells initiate ABA and osmotic responses.

tissue.zh: 叶片蒸腾开始下降，而含水状态大致维持。
tissue.en: Leaf transpiration begins declining while hydration remains largely maintained.

trait.zh: 叶片外观基本正常，生长可能轻微放缓。
trait.en: Leaves remain visually normal, with possible slight growth slowing.

text.zh: 实际叶片水势是主要缺失条件。
text.en: Actual leaf water potential is the main missing condition.

state.zh: RD29A关联应激转录增强并偏离充分供水基线。
state.en: RD29A-associated stress transcription strengthens and diverges from the well-watered baseline.

cell.zh: 细胞加强渗透保护并限制失水。
cell.en: Cells strengthen osmoprotection and water-loss limitation.

tissue.zh: 气孔导度下降，光合可能开始受限。
tissue.en: Stomatal conductance decreases, and photosynthesis may become constrained.

trait.zh: 叶片生长减慢，但萎蔫仍不明显。
trait.en: Leaf growth slows, but wilting remains inconspicuous.

text.zh: 起始昼夜节律相位尚未指定。
text.en: The initial circadian phase remains unspecified.

state.zh: RD29A关联状态转为明显的脱水应激程序。
state.en: The RD29A-associated state becomes a distinct dehydration stress program.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞强化保护性转录与氧化还原调节。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells reinforce protective transcription and redox regulation.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分亏缺加深，碳同化进一步下降。
tissue.en: Leaf water deficit deepens, and carbon assimilation declines further.

trait.zh: 轻度萎蔫或叶片角度变化可能出现。
trait.en: Mild wilting or altered leaf angle may appear.

text.zh: 蒸气压亏缺是主要环境不确定性。
text.en: Vapor pressure deficit is the main environmental uncertainty.

state.zh: 复水时RD29A关联脱水状态仍强，随后才可能衰减。
state.en: At rewatering, the RD29A-associated dehydration state remains strong before any decline.

cell.zh: 细胞保护程序活跃，而膨压恢复尚未完成。
cell.en: Cellular protection programs remain active before turgor recovery is complete.

tissue.zh: 叶片水势较低且气孔大多关闭。
tissue.en: Leaf water potential is low, with stomata largely closed.

trait.zh: 可见萎蔫可能最明显，但仍可逆。
trait.en: Visible wilting may be strongest yet remain reversible.

text.zh: 取样相对复水操作的准确时点不明确。
text.en: The exact sampling time relative to rewatering is unclear.

state.zh: RD29A关联转录开始回落，但保留明显应激历史。
state.en: RD29A-associated transcription begins declining while retaining a clear stress history.

cell.zh: 细胞膨压恢复快于应激转录的重置。
cell.en: Cell turgor recovers faster than stress transcription resets.

tissue.zh: 叶片含水量回升，但气孔开放仍滞后。
tissue.en: Leaf hydration rises, but stomatal reopening still lags.

trait.zh: 萎蔫减轻，叶片姿态开始恢复。
trait.en: Wilting eases, and leaf posture begins recovering.

text.zh: 根系吸水恢复速率是主要未知因素。
text.en: The rate of restored root water uptake is the main unknown.

state.zh: RD29A关联应激程序继续减弱，但未完全归零。
state.en: The RD29A-associated stress program continues weakening without fully returning to baseline.

cell.zh: ABA信号减退，而修复和氧化还原调节持续。
cell.en: ABA signaling subsides while repair and redox regulation persist.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分接近恢复，光合恢复可能较慢。
tissue.en: Leaf hydration nears recovery, while photosynthetic recovery may remain slower.

trait.zh: 叶片外观明显恢复，但生长仍受前期限制。
trait.en: Leaf appearance improves markedly, but growth still reflects earlier restriction.

text.zh: ABA清除速度是主要缺失条件。
text.en: The rate of ABA clearance is the main missing condition.

state.zh: RD29A关联表达接近低残留状态。
state.en: RD29A-associated expression approaches a low residual state.

cell.zh: 细胞转向稳态恢复，并保留部分保护能力。
cell.en: Cells shift toward homeostatic recovery while retaining some protective capacity.

tissue.zh: 气孔导度和光合逐步接近充分供水水平。
tissue.en: Stomatal conductance and photosynthesis progressively approach well-watered levels.

trait.zh: 萎蔫基本消失，但叶片扩展可能仍滞后。
trait.en: Wilting largely disappears, though leaf expansion may still lag.

text.zh: 气孔重新开放的程度仍不清楚。
text.en: The extent of stomatal reopening remains unclear.

state.zh: RD29A关联状态接近基线，但可能保留转录记忆。
state.en: The RD29A-associated state nears baseline but may retain transcriptional memory.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞恢复代谢，同时维持轻度防护准备。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells restore metabolism while maintaining mild protective readiness.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合大致恢复，局部损伤可能持续。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthesis is largely restored, though localized damage may persist.

trait.zh: 生长恢复，但累计叶面积可能略低。
trait.en: Growth resumes, but cumulative leaf area may remain slightly reduced.

text.zh: 复水前光抑制程度是主要未知因素。
text.en: The pre-rewatering degree of photoinhibition is the main unknown.

state.zh: RD29A关联活动接近基础水平，残留敏感性可能存在。
state.en: RD29A-associated activity approaches basal levels, with possible residual sensitivity.

cell.zh: 细胞稳态占主导，但再胁迫反应阈值可能改变。
cell.en: Cellular homeostasis dominates, though the threshold for renewed stress responses may differ.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分交换稳定，修复过程接近完成。
tissue.en: Leaf water exchange stabilizes, and repair processes approach completion.

trait.zh: 叶片不再萎蔫，但早期生长损失可能保留。
trait.en: Leaves no longer wilt, though earlier growth loss may remain.

text.zh: 叶龄差异最影响残留状态判断。
text.en: Leaf-age variation most affects interpretation of the residual state.

state.zh: RD29A关联状态维持近基线并带有轻度适应痕迹。
state.en: The RD29A-associated state remains near baseline with mild acclimation traces.

cell.zh: 细胞防护与生长程序重新取得平衡。
cell.en: Cellular protection and growth programs regain balance.

tissue.zh: 叶片气体交换稳定，但可能未完全等同对照。
tissue.en: Leaf gas exchange stabilizes but may not fully match controls.

trait.zh: 外观恢复正常，叶片大小差异可能持续。
trait.en: Appearance normalizes, while leaf-size differences may persist.

text.zh: 叶片间初始干旱差异仍是主要歧义。
text.en: Initial drought variation among leaves remains the main ambiguity.

state.zh: RD29A关联转录大致稳定于恢复后的低水平。
state.en: RD29A-associated transcription stabilizes at a low post-recovery level.

cell.zh: 细胞主要维持稳态，仅保留有限应激准备。
cell.en: Cells mainly maintain homeostasis with limited residual stress readiness.

tissue.zh: 叶片功能接近对照，但组织历史仍可能可测。
tissue.en: Leaf function approaches controls, though tissue history may remain measurable.

trait.zh: 持续萎蔫不太可能，生长差距可能仍在。
trait.en: Persistent wilting is unlikely, while a growth gap may remain.

text.zh: 复水后的根区通气状况尚未给出。
text.en: Root-zone aeration after rewatering is not specified.

state.zh: RD29A关联状态接近基线，但不排除干旱遗留效应。
state.en: The RD29A-associated state approaches baseline without excluding drought legacy effects.

cell.zh: 细胞稳态恢复，潜在应激记忆可能仍可诱发。
cell.en: Cellular homeostasis is restored, while latent stress memory may remain inducible.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分与光合功能总体稳定。
tissue.en: Leaf hydration and photosynthetic function are broadly stable.

trait.zh: 叶片外观正常，但最终生物量可能略受影响。
trait.en: Leaves appear normal, though final biomass may remain modestly affected.

text.zh: 应激记忆持续时间是主要未知条件。
text.en: The persistence of stress memory is the main unknown condition.

readouts.zh: 用RT-qPCR或报告基因测量RD29A时间序列表达。
readouts.en: Measure RD29A expression over time using RT-qPCR or a validated reporter.

readouts.zh: 同步测量叶片水势、相对含水量和膨压恢复。
readouts.en: Measure leaf water potential, relative water content, and turgor recovery concurrently.

readouts.zh: 记录气孔导度、蒸腾和叶温。
readouts.en: Record stomatal conductance, transpiration, and leaf temperature.

readouts.zh: 测量叶绿素荧光、碳同化和氧化还原指标。
readouts.en: Measure chlorophyll fluorescence, carbon assimilation, and redox indicators.

controls.zh: 设置全程充分供水且昼夜相位匹配的对照。
controls.en: Include continuously watered controls matched for circadian phase.

controls.zh: 设置干旱后不复水的平行对照。
controls.en: Include a parallel drought treatment without rewatering.

controls.zh: 在复水前后设置高密度取样对照。
controls.en: Use dense sampling immediately before and after rewatering.

title.zh: RD29A关联的叶片冷驯化轨迹

geneFunction.zh: RD29A是脱水与冷响应程序的状态探针，并不证明其因果作用。
geneFunction.en: RD29A probes dehydration-linked cold responses without establishing causal control.

state.zh: RD29A转录可能迅速升高，反映早期ABA与CBF/DREB信号汇合。
state.en: RD29A transcription likely rises rapidly as early ABA and CBF/DREB signaling converge.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞启动渗透调节、膜保护与氧化还原缓冲。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells initiate osmotic adjustment, membrane protection, and redox buffering.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合作用开始受抑，但组织损伤预计有限。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthesis begins slowing, while tissue injury should remain limited.

trait.zh: 植株通常尚无清晰可见变化。
trait.en: Plants likely show no clear visible change yet.

text.zh: 实际叶温是早期响应幅度的主要未知条件。
text.en: Actual leaf temperature is the main unknown governing early response magnitude.

state.zh: RD29A信号可能接近早期高位，并受光周期相位调节。
state.en: RD29A signaling may approach an early high state modulated by photoperiod phase.

cell.zh: 细胞增加保护性转录，并重新分配碳与还原力。
cell.en: Cells increase protective transcription and redistribute carbon and reducing power.

tissue.zh: 叶片气孔与光合调节趋于保守，以限制冷诱导失衡。
tissue.en: Leaf stomatal and photosynthetic regulation becomes conservative to limit cold-induced imbalance.

trait.zh: 叶片外观多半保持正常，生长速度可能开始下降。
trait.en: Leaves likely appear normal, although growth may begin slowing.

text.zh: 未给定初始昼夜相位，限制峰值时序判断。
text.en: Unspecified initial circadian phase limits prediction of peak timing.

state.zh: RD29A维持诱导，但幅度可能随昼夜输入波动。
state.en: RD29A remains induced, but its amplitude may fluctuate with diel inputs.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞积累兼容溶质并加强膜脂稳态。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells accumulate compatible solutes and reinforce membrane-lipid homeostasis.

tissue.zh: 叶片代谢转向维持与防护，扩展活动进一步减慢。
tissue.en: Leaf metabolism shifts toward maintenance and protection as expansion slows further.

trait.zh: 可见伤害仍不明显，但新生长可能受到抑制。
trait.en: Visible injury remains unlikely, but new growth may be restrained.

text.zh: 叶龄差异可能改变冷响应的强度。
text.en: Leaf age may substantially alter cold-response strength.

state.zh: RD29A可能进入持续高表达或初步适应性平台。
state.en: RD29A may enter sustained high expression or an early acclimatory plateau.

cell.zh: 细胞稳定渗透压，并增强蛋白质与膜系统保护。
cell.en: Cells stabilize osmotic status and strengthen protein and membrane protection.

tissue.zh: 叶片逐步重设光合容量与呼吸需求。
tissue.en: Leaves progressively reset photosynthetic capacity and respiratory demand.

trait.zh: 整株生长减慢可能开始可辨，但叶片仍具活力。
trait.en: Whole-plant growth slowing may become discernible while leaves remain viable.

text.zh: 冷处理温度决定驯化与损伤的平衡。
text.en: Cold-treatment temperature determines the balance between acclimation and injury.

state.zh: RD29A持续高于基线，但反馈可能限制进一步升高。
state.en: RD29A stays above baseline, while feedback may limit further elevation.

cell.zh: 细胞由急性信号转向稳态防护与资源节约。
cell.en: Cells transition from acute signaling toward steady protection and resource conservation.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持降低的碳同化，并减少生长投入。
tissue.en: Leaves maintain reduced carbon assimilation and lower investment in growth.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展减慢可能比颜色变化更明显。
trait.en: Slower leaf expansion may be more evident than color change.

text.zh: 空气湿度会改变脱水成分及RD29A信号。
text.en: Air humidity may alter the dehydration component and RD29A signal.

state.zh: RD29A可能在高位平台上呈相位相关波动。
state.en: RD29A may show phase-linked fluctuations around an elevated plateau.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞维持抗氧化能力与膜流动性调节。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells maintain antioxidant capacity and membrane-fluidity adjustment.

tissue.zh: 叶片光系统趋于稳定，但性能低于温暖基线。
tissue.en: Leaf photosystems become more stable but perform below the warm baseline.

trait.zh: 植株呈现较慢生长，而非明显坏死。
trait.en: Plants show slower growth rather than overt necrosis.

text.zh: 光强决定冷下光氧化压力的大小。
text.en: Light intensity determines photoxidative pressure during cold exposure.

state.zh: RD29A仍标记持续冷响应，急性成分可能减弱。
state.en: RD29A still marks persistent cold response as acute components may weaken.

cell.zh: 细胞保护程序稳定，并限制非必要合成活动。
cell.en: Cellular protection stabilizes while nonessential biosynthesis remains constrained.

tissue.zh: 叶片在受限光合与维持代谢间形成新平衡。
tissue.en: Leaves establish a new balance between constrained photosynthesis and maintenance metabolism.

trait.zh: 叶片生长滞后可能累积，但结构完整性仍保持。
trait.en: Leaf growth delay may accumulate while structural integrity remains preserved.

text.zh: 营养状态会影响维持代谢的持续能力。
text.en: Nutrient status may affect the durability of maintenance metabolism.

state.zh: RD29A预计保持诱导，并受反馈与昼夜节律共同塑形。
state.en: RD29A likely remains induced under combined feedback and diel modulation.

cell.zh: 细胞持续渗透保护，并控制活性氧负荷。
cell.en: Cells sustain osmoprotection and control reactive oxygen burden.

tissue.zh: 叶片功能维持在驯化后的低通量状态。
tissue.en: Leaf function persists in an acclimated, lower-throughput state.

trait.zh: 整株可能更紧凑，叶片展开速度继续降低。
trait.en: Plants may become more compact as leaf unfolding remains slow.

text.zh: 未测组织含水量限制脱水信号的解释。
text.en: Unmeasured tissue water status limits interpretation of dehydration signaling.

state.zh: RD29A高于基线的状态可能持续而不再单调增强。
state.en: RD29A may remain above baseline without continuing to rise monotonically.

cell.zh: 细胞优先维持膜、蛋白质和氧化还原稳态。
cell.en: Cells prioritize membrane, protein, and redox homeostasis.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合装置可能部分适应持续低温。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthetic machinery may partially acclimate to sustained cold.

trait.zh: 生长抑制逐渐可见，但严重萎蔫并非必然。
trait.en: Growth inhibition becomes clearer, but severe wilting is not expected.

text.zh: 基因型背景可能改变RD29A诱导阈值。
text.en: Genetic background may shift the threshold for RD29A induction.

state.zh: RD29A维持驯化相关信号，并可能出现周期性起伏。
state.en: RD29A maintains an acclimation-associated signal with possible periodic variation.

cell.zh: 细胞保持防护能力，同时压低生长相关翻译。
cell.en: Cells retain protection while suppressing growth-associated translation.

tissue.zh: 叶片代谢稳定，但碳增益仍受冷限制。
tissue.en: Leaf metabolism stabilizes, although carbon gain remains cold-limited.

trait.zh: 叶片面积增长落后于温暖对照。
trait.en: Leaf-area expansion falls behind the warm control.

text.zh: 昼夜采样相位是周期波动解释的关键。
text.en: Diel sampling phase is critical for interpreting periodic variation.

state.zh: RD29A可能保持中高水平，显示持续而非急性胁迫。
state.en: RD29A may remain moderately high, indicating persistent rather than acute stress.

cell.zh: 细胞维持冷适应稳态，并减少额外应激投入。
cell.en: Cells maintain cold-acclimated homeostasis while limiting additional stress investment.

tissue.zh: 叶片功能趋稳，损伤与修复保持动态平衡。
tissue.en: Leaf function stabilizes as injury and repair remain dynamically balanced.

trait.zh: 植株生长持续偏慢，叶色可能基本保持。
trait.en: Plant growth remains slow while leaf color may stay largely preserved.

text.zh: 持续冷下的光周期强度决定组织损伤风险。
text.en: Photoperiod intensity during sustained cold determines tissue-injury risk.

state.zh: RD29A预计形成持续诱导的平台，并保留相位依赖性。
state.en: RD29A likely forms a sustained induced plateau retaining phase dependence.

cell.zh: 细胞保持渗透、膜与氧化还原防护的综合状态。
cell.en: Cells preserve integrated osmotic, membrane, and redox protection.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持驯化后的低生长、可存活功能状态。
tissue.en: Leaves maintain a viable, acclimated state with reduced growth.

trait.zh: 最终表型更可能是生长延缓，而非急性叶片死亡。
trait.en: The likely phenotype is growth delay rather than acute leaf death.

text.zh: 是否发生冻点附近波动是终点判断的主要未知。
text.en: Possible near-freezing fluctuations are the main uncertainty for the endpoint.

readouts.zh: 按光周期密集测量RD29A转录与启动子报告信号。
readouts.en: Measure RD29A transcripts and promoter-reporter activity densely across the photoperiod.

readouts.zh: 同步测量ABA、CBF靶基因及叶片含水状态。
readouts.en: Measure ABA, CBF targets, and leaf water status at matched times.

readouts.zh: 测定叶绿素荧光、电解质渗漏、气孔导度和叶面积增长。
readouts.en: Quantify chlorophyll fluorescence, electrolyte leakage, stomatal conductance, and leaf-area growth.

controls.zh: 使用同基因型、同叶龄、同光周期相位的温暖对照。
controls.en: Use warm controls matched for genotype, leaf age, and photoperiod phase.

controls.zh: 设置相同低温但不同湿度的对照以分离脱水成分。
controls.en: Include cold controls at matched temperatures but contrasting humidity to separate dehydration effects.

controls.zh: 比较ABA或CBF通路受扰材料，但仅用于检验关联机制。
controls.en: Compare ABA- or CBF-pathway perturbations solely to test associated mechanisms.

title.zh: 该轨迹以TOC1为探针描述正常供水叶片的昼夜状态。
title.en: This trajectory uses TOC1 as a probe of circadian state in well-watered leaves.

geneFunction.zh: TOC1参与昼夜振荡及环境响应与生长的时相协调。
geneFunction.en: TOC1 participates in circadian oscillation and phase-dependent coordination of environmental responses with growth.

state.zh: TOC1关联振荡器约推进四分之一周期，但绝对时相未知。
state.en: The TOC1-associated oscillator advances roughly one-quarter cycle, while its absolute phase remains unknown.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞切换受时钟门控的光合与生长程序。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells shift clock-gated photosynthetic and growth programs.

tissue.zh: 叶片在充足水分下维持协调的昼夜代谢。
tissue.en: The hydrated leaf maintains coordinated diel metabolism.

trait.zh: 预计叶片生长正常，未出现水分胁迫性状。
trait.en: Normal leaf growth is expected without water-stress traits.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相未知，无法判定TOC1高低状态。
text.en: The initial Zeitgeber phase is unknown, preventing assignment of high or low TOC1 state.

state.zh: 振荡器约到达与起点相对的时相区段。
state.en: The oscillator reaches a phase sector roughly opposite the starting state.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞偏向互补的昼夜转录与碳利用程序。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells favor complementary circadian transcription and carbon-use programs.

tissue.zh: 叶片代谢随光周期在碳获取与利用间转换。
tissue.en: Leaf metabolism transitions between carbon acquisition and utilization with the photoperiod.

trait.zh: 叶片运动与扩展应保持正常日变化。
trait.en: Leaf movement and expansion should retain normal daily variation.

text.zh: 缺少初始授时因子时相，具体昼夜区段仍不确定。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase leaves the specific circadian sector unresolved.

state.zh: 振荡器约推进四分之三周期并接近首轮回归。
state.en: The oscillator advances roughly three-quarters of a cycle and approaches its first recurrence.

cell.zh: 细胞时钟准备重置下一轮代谢与生长门控。
cell.en: Cellular clocks prepare the next round of metabolic and growth gating.

tissue.zh: 叶片各区域预计保持相近但非完全一致的节律。
tissue.en: Leaf regions should remain similarly rhythmic without perfect synchrony.

trait.zh: 整体外观预计稳定，仅呈正常昼夜波动。
trait.en: Overall appearance should remain stable with only normal diel fluctuations.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相仍是判断当前状态的主要缺口。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber phase remains the main gap for identifying the current state.

state.zh: 振荡器应回到近似起始时相，但会有噪声与发育漂移。
state.en: The oscillator should revisit a starting-like phase with biological noise and developmental drift.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞重复相似的时钟门控程序。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells repeat a broadly similar clock-gated program.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持跨日协调的光合与碳分配节律。
tissue.en: The leaf maintains day-to-day coordination of photosynthesis and carbon allocation.

trait.zh: 叶片生长轨迹预计接近正常供水基线。
trait.en: Leaf growth should remain near the well-watered baseline.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相未知，限制了回归状态的精确定位。
text.en: Unknown initial Zeitgeber phase limits precise placement of the recurrent state.

state.zh: 第二轮振荡约推进四分之一周期，绝对时相仍未定。
state.en: The second oscillation advances roughly one-quarter cycle, with absolute phase still unresolved.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞再次切换光合、氧化还原与生长门控。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells again shift photosynthetic, redox, and growth gating.

tissue.zh: 叶片继续维持水分充足下的昼夜代谢协调。
tissue.en: The leaf continues coordinated diel metabolism under adequate water.

trait.zh: 预计无持续萎蔫或生长抑制。
trait.en: Persistent wilting or growth inhibition is not expected.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相缺失，妨碍确定TOC1所处峰谷。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase prevents locating TOC1 near a peak or trough.

state.zh: 第二轮振荡约进入与该轮起点相对的时相区段。
state.en: The second oscillation enters a phase sector roughly opposite its cycle start.

cell.zh: 细胞转向互补的碳代谢与扩展程序。
cell.en: Cells shift toward complementary carbon-metabolic and expansion programs.

tissue.zh: 叶片的气孔与光合活动保持时钟和光照共同门控。
tissue.en: Leaf stomatal and photosynthetic activity remains jointly gated by clock and light.

trait.zh: 叶片角度和扩展速率应呈正常周期变化。
trait.en: Leaf angle and expansion rate should show normal periodic variation.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相未知，限制对当前门控方向的判断。
text.en: Unknown initial Zeitgeber phase limits inference of the current gating direction.

state.zh: 第二轮振荡约推进四分之三周期并趋向再次回归。
state.en: The second oscillation advances roughly three-quarters of a cycle and approaches recurrence.

cell.zh: 细胞时钟协调下一轮转录与代谢转换。
cell.en: Cellular clocks coordinate the next transcriptional and metabolic transition.

tissue.zh: 叶片节律预计保持同步，但局部相位可能轻微分散。
tissue.en: Leaf rhythms should remain coordinated, although local phases may disperse slightly.

trait.zh: 可见性状预计稳定并保持正常昼夜节律。
trait.en: Visible traits should remain stable and normally rhythmic.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相仍未提供，绝对状态无法指定。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber phase remains unavailable, so the absolute state cannot be assigned.

state.zh: 振荡器应第二次接近起始时相，并累积轻微发育漂移。
state.en: The oscillator should approach the starting phase again with modest accumulated developmental drift.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞重现相似但不完全相同的时钟状态。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells reproduce a similar but not identical clock state.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持跨两日的光合与资源分配协调。
tissue.en: The leaf maintains photosynthetic and resource-allocation coordination across two days.

trait.zh: 叶片大小与活力预计仍符合正常供水状态。
trait.en: Leaf size and vigor should remain consistent with adequate watering.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相缺失，妨碍验证跨日相位回归。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase hinders verification of day-to-day phase recurrence.

state.zh: 第三轮振荡约推进四分之一周期，仍不可指定峰谷。
state.en: The third oscillation advances roughly one-quarter cycle, while peaks and troughs remain unassignable.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞再次更新受时钟门控的光合与生长状态。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells again update clock-gated photosynthetic and growth states.

tissue.zh: 叶片继续表现稳定的昼夜代谢组织。
tissue.en: The leaf continues stable diel metabolic organization.

trait.zh: 预计保持正常扩展且不出现缺水表型。
trait.en: Normal expansion should continue without water-deficit phenotypes.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相未知，无法把该状态对应到明暗期。
text.en: Unknown initial Zeitgeber phase prevents mapping this state to light or dark.

state.zh: 第三轮振荡约进入与该轮起点相对的时相区段。
state.en: The third oscillation enters a phase sector roughly opposite its cycle start.

cell.zh: 细胞采用互补的能量利用和生长门控模式。
cell.en: Cells adopt complementary energy-use and growth-gating modes.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持气体交换与碳分配的周期协调。
tissue.en: The leaf maintains periodic coordination of gas exchange and carbon allocation.

trait.zh: 叶片运动和生长速率应继续正常振荡。
trait.en: Leaf movement and growth rate should continue normal oscillation.

text.zh: 缺少初始授时因子时相，当前转录方向仍有歧义。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase leaves the current transcriptional direction ambiguous.

state.zh: 第三轮振荡约推进四分之三周期并接近末次回归。
state.en: The third oscillation advances roughly three-quarters of a cycle and approaches the final recurrence.

cell.zh: 细胞时钟准备再次进入相似的周期起始状态。
cell.en: Cellular clocks prepare to re-enter a similar cycle-starting state.

tissue.zh: 叶片整体节律预计持续，局部同步性可能略降。
tissue.en: Whole-leaf rhythmicity should persist, with slightly reduced local synchrony possible.

trait.zh: 整体叶片性状预计仍接近基线。
trait.en: Whole-leaf traits should remain near baseline.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相未知，限制末次回归的相位解释。
text.en: Unknown initial Zeitgeber phase limits interpretation of the final recurrence.

state.zh: 振荡器应第三次接近起始时相，并保留发育相关漂移。
state.en: The oscillator should approach the starting phase a third time while retaining developmental drift.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞保持周期性但随叶龄缓慢调整。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells remain rhythmic while adjusting gradually with leaf age.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持三日尺度的代谢与生长协调。
tissue.en: The leaf maintains metabolic and growth coordination across three days.

trait.zh: 最终叶片状态预计健康且接近正常供水基线。
trait.en: The final leaf state should remain healthy and near the well-watered baseline.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时相缺失，仍无法确认绝对昼夜相位。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase still prevents confirmation of absolute circadian phase.

readouts.zh: 每隔数小时测量TOC1转录本或启动子报告信号。
readouts.en: Measure TOC1 transcript or promoter-reporter signal at multi-hour intervals.

readouts.zh: 同步检测CCA1、LHY和PRR7等相位标记。
readouts.en: Measure phase markers such as CCA1, LHY, and PRR7 concurrently.

readouts.zh: 连续记录叶绿素荧光、气体交换和气孔导度。
readouts.en: Track chlorophyll fluorescence, gas exchange, and stomatal conductance continuously.

readouts.zh: 成像量化叶片角度、扩展速率与昼夜运动。
readouts.en: Use imaging to quantify leaf angle, expansion rate, and diel movement.

controls.zh: 使用同基因型、同叶龄且时间匹配的正常供水叶片。
controls.en: Use time-matched, same-genotype, same-age leaves under identical watering.

controls.zh: 监测土壤含水量以确认全程供水稳定。
controls.en: Monitor soil moisture to verify stable watering throughout.

controls.zh: 记录明暗转换和温度以确定实际授时条件。
controls.en: Record light transitions and temperature to establish actual Zeitgeber conditions.

title.zh: TOC1关联的叶片持续干旱状态轨迹
title.en: TOC1-associated leaf trajectory under sustained drought

geneFunction.zh: TOC1反映昼夜振荡器相位及其与胁迫和生长的协调状态。
geneFunction.en: TOC1 reports circadian phase and its coordination with stress responses and growth.

state.zh: TOC1仍主要反映基线振荡，相位决定短时方向。
state.en: TOC1 mainly reflects baseline oscillation, with phase determining the short-term direction.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞和叶肉细胞开始感知轻微水分下降。
cell.en: Guard and mesophyll cells begin sensing a slight decline in water availability.

tissue.zh: 叶片含水与光合结构预计仍接近基线。
tissue.en: Leaf hydration and photosynthetic structure likely remain near baseline.

trait.zh: 植株外观预计尚无明确变化。
trait.en: Plant appearance likely shows no clear change yet.

text.zh: 缺少初始授时因子时相，无法判定TOC1方向。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber phase is missing, preventing directional interpretation of TOC1.

state.zh: TOC1节律开始与早期干旱信号叠加。
state.en: TOC1 rhythmic state begins integrating with early drought signaling.

cell.zh: 气孔调节增强，叶肉氧化还原负荷轻度上升。
cell.en: Stomatal regulation strengthens as mesophyll redox load rises slightly.

tissue.zh: 蒸腾受限可能先于叶片失水表现。
tissue.en: Transpiration restriction may precede evident leaf dehydration.

trait.zh: 可见生长差异仍可能很小。
trait.en: Visible growth differences may remain minor.

text.zh: 初始昼夜相位仍是解释TOC1变化的主要歧义。
text.en: Initial circadian phase remains the main ambiguity in interpreting TOC1 changes.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态提示时钟与脱落酸响应正在协调。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state suggests coordination between the clock and abscisic acid responses.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞趋向关闭，叶肉碳同化开始受限。
cell.en: Guard cells trend toward closure while mesophyll carbon assimilation becomes constrained.

tissue.zh: 叶片水势下降并出现轻度光合限制。
tissue.en: Leaf water potential declines with mild photosynthetic limitation.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展可能开始放缓。
trait.en: Leaf expansion may begin slowing.

text.zh: 未知授时相位限制了时钟与干旱效应的分离。
text.en: Unknown Zeitgeber phase limits separation of clock and drought effects.

state.zh: TOC1振荡可能被持续干旱重塑，但方向依赖相位。
state.en: Sustained drought may reshape TOC1 oscillation, although direction remains phase dependent.

cell.zh: 气孔关闭与渗透调节在多类叶细胞中增强。
cell.en: Stomatal closure and osmotic adjustment strengthen across leaf cell types.

tissue.zh: 叶片失水与光合下降变得可测。
tissue.en: Leaf dehydration and photosynthetic decline become measurable.

trait.zh: 生长减缓可能先于明显萎蔫。
trait.en: Growth slowing may precede obvious wilting.

text.zh: 缺少初始授时相位，无法区分相移与表达幅度变化。
text.en: Missing initial Zeitgeber phase prevents distinguishing phase shifts from amplitude changes.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态进入反复时钟门控的胁迫响应。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state enters recurrent clock-gated stress responsiveness.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞加强抗氧化与渗透保护程序。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells strengthen antioxidant and osmoprotective programs.

tissue.zh: 叶片导度下降并限制碳获取。
tissue.en: Leaf conductance falls and restricts carbon acquisition.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展持续减慢，午间下垂可能出现。
trait.en: Leaf expansion continues slowing, and midday drooping may appear.

text.zh: 初始昼夜相位未知，使门控响应时序难以定位。
text.en: Unknown initial circadian phase obscures the timing of gated responses.

state.zh: TOC1节律可能出现相位偏移或振幅改变。
state.en: TOC1 rhythmicity may show a phase shift or altered amplitude.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞维持低导度，叶肉能量平衡承压。
cell.en: Guard cells maintain low conductance while mesophyll energy balance becomes strained.

tissue.zh: 持续低水势开始影响叶片昼夜碳平衡。
tissue.en: Persistently low water potential begins altering daily leaf carbon balance.

trait.zh: 生物量积累预计明显放缓。
trait.en: Biomass accumulation is expected to slow clearly.

text.zh: 未指定授时相位，无法确定TOC1节律改变的类型。
text.en: Unspecified Zeitgeber phase prevents identifying the type of TOC1 rhythm alteration.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态提示时钟与代谢胁迫耦合加深。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state suggests deeper coupling between clock and metabolic stress.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞降低碳同化并加强损伤防护。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells reduce carbon assimilation and reinforce damage protection.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分亏缺与光合抑制进一步累积。
tissue.en: Leaf water deficit and photosynthetic suppression accumulate further.

trait.zh: 叶片下垂与扩展停滞更可能可见。
trait.en: Leaf drooping and expansion arrest become more likely visible.

text.zh: 初始相位缺失仍妨碍判断时钟重排程度。
text.en: Missing initial phase still hinders assessment of clock reorganization.

state.zh: TOC1振荡可能减弱或失同步，而非单向改变。
state.en: TOC1 oscillation may dampen or desynchronize rather than change monotonically.

cell.zh: 细胞渗透保护增强，但氧化与能量压力持续。
cell.en: Cellular osmoprotection strengthens, but oxidative and energetic pressures persist.

tissue.zh: 叶片日变化幅度可能缩小并伴随功能受限。
tissue.en: Daily leaf fluctuations may narrow alongside functional limitation.

trait.zh: 持续生长抑制与轻度萎蔫可能并存。
trait.en: Persistent growth suppression and mild wilting may coexist.

text.zh: 未知授时相位使振幅减弱与细胞失同步难以区分。
text.en: Unknown Zeitgeber phase makes damping difficult to distinguish from cellular desynchrony.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态转向持续胁迫下的节律适应。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state shifts toward rhythmic accommodation under persistent stress.

cell.zh: 存活细胞维持低生长和高保护投入。
cell.en: Surviving cells maintain low growth and high protective investment.

tissue.zh: 叶片资源分配偏向维持而非碳增益。
tissue.en: Leaf resource allocation favors maintenance over carbon gain.

trait.zh: 叶片面积增长受限，萎蔫风险上升。
trait.en: Leaf area growth remains restricted as wilting risk rises.

text.zh: 缺少初始相位，适应性节律重排仍无法定向。
text.en: Missing initial phase prevents directional assignment of adaptive rhythmic reorganization.

state.zh: TOC1可能标记低振幅但仍受相位门控的状态。
state.en: TOC1 may mark a lower-amplitude state that remains phase gated.

cell.zh: 叶肉代谢降低，抗氧化和修复需求维持。
cell.en: Mesophyll metabolism declines while antioxidant and repair demands persist.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合能力与含水状态保持受抑。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthetic capacity and hydration remain suppressed.

trait.zh: 生长近乎停滞，持续下垂更可能出现。
trait.en: Growth may approach arrest, with persistent drooping becoming more likely.

text.zh: 初始授时相位未知，低振幅解释仍具歧义。
text.en: Unknown initial Zeitgeber phase leaves the lower-amplitude interpretation ambiguous.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态反映时钟维持与胁迫耐受的权衡。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state reflects a tradeoff between clock maintenance and stress tolerance.

cell.zh: 细胞优先维持稳态，并进一步压低合成代谢。
cell.en: Cells prioritize homeostasis while further suppressing anabolic metabolism.

tissue.zh: 叶片功能下降可能接近可逆性边界。
tissue.en: Leaf functional decline may approach a reversibility threshold.

trait.zh: 明显萎蔫和生长停滞风险增加。
trait.en: Risks of pronounced wilting and growth arrest increase.

text.zh: 缺少叶片水势使胁迫严重度边界难以判断。
text.en: Missing leaf water potential makes the stress-severity boundary difficult to assess.

state.zh: TOC1可能维持重塑后的节律状态或出现明显失同步。
state.en: TOC1 may retain a remodeled rhythm or show substantial desynchronization.

cell.zh: 细胞维持保护程序，部分叶肉功能可能受损。
cell.en: Cells sustain protective programs, while some mesophyll functions may become impaired.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分和碳平衡处于持续受限状态。
tissue.en: Leaf water and carbon balances remain persistently constrained.

trait.zh: 植株表现为强烈生长抑制并可能明显萎蔫。
trait.en: Plants show strong growth suppression and may display pronounced wilting.

text.zh: 初始相位与实际水势均影响终点节律解释。
text.en: Initial phase and actual water potential jointly limit endpoint rhythm interpretation.

readouts.zh: 连续测量TOC1转录或报告信号以估计相位和振幅。
readouts.en: Track TOC1 transcript or reporter dynamics continuously to estimate phase and amplitude.

readouts.zh: 同步测量叶片水势、相对含水量与气孔导度。
readouts.en: Measure leaf water potential, relative water content, and stomatal conductance concurrently.

readouts.zh: 监测叶绿素荧光、气体交换与叶片温度。
readouts.en: Monitor chlorophyll fluorescence, gas exchange, and leaf temperature.

readouts.zh: 量化叶面积增长、叶角和萎蔫程度。
readouts.en: Quantify leaf area growth, leaf angle, and wilting severity.

controls.zh: 设置同授时相位、同取样时刻的充分供水对照。
controls.en: Use well-watered controls matched for Zeitgeber phase and sampling time.

controls.zh: 记录初始授时因子时并保持光温周期一致。
controls.en: Record initial Zeitgeber time and maintain identical light and temperature cycles.

controls.zh: 设置匹配叶位、发育阶段和基因型的对照。
controls.en: Match leaf position, developmental stage, and genotype across conditions.

title.zh: TOC1关联的叶片干旱—复水状态轨迹。
title.en: TOC1-associated leaf state trajectory through drought and rewatering.

geneFunction.zh: TOC1连接昼夜振荡器与环境响应，并依时相协调叶片生长。
geneFunction.en: TOC1 links the circadian oscillator with environmental responses and coordinates leaf growth in a phase-dependent manner.

state.zh: 早期脱水信号可能接入TOC1时钟输出，其方向取决于昼夜时相。
state.en: Early dehydration signals may intersect TOC1 clock output, with direction contingent on circadian phase.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞与叶肉细胞开始调整ABA、氧化还原及生长信号。
cell.en: Guard and mesophyll cells begin adjusting ABA, redox, and growth signaling.

tissue.zh: 叶片气孔导度可能轻降，而整体水合尚接近起始状态。
tissue.en: Leaf conductance may decline slightly while overall hydration remains near the starting state.

trait.zh: 可见萎蔫预计很少，但叶片扩展可能开始放缓。
trait.en: Visible wilting is expected to be minimal, although leaf expansion may begin slowing.

text.zh: 最关键缺失条件是处理开始时的昼夜时相。
text.en: The key missing condition is circadian phase when treatment begins.

state.zh: 持续失水可能增强TOC1关联的时钟门控，但无法确定表达升降。
state.en: Continued water loss may strengthen TOC1-associated clock gating, but expression direction cannot be assigned.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞趋向关闭，叶肉细胞逐步限制碳同化与生长活动。
cell.en: Guard cells tend toward closure while mesophyll cells progressively restrict carbon assimilation and growth.

tissue.zh: 叶片蒸腾下降，水势与光合协调开始偏离充足供水基线。
tissue.en: Leaf transpiration falls as water potential and photosynthetic coordination diverge from the watered baseline.

trait.zh: 叶片生长减慢，轻微失挺可能出现但未必可见。
trait.en: Leaf growth slows, and mild loss of firmness may emerge without obvious wilting.

text.zh: 起始昼夜时相未知，限制了TOC1响应方向的判断。
text.en: Unknown starting circadian phase limits inference of TOC1 response direction.

state.zh: 干旱状态更明确，TOC1关联输出可能重排胁迫与生长程序的时序。
state.en: Drought state becomes clearer, and TOC1-associated output may reorder stress and growth programs.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞关闭增强，叶肉细胞积累胁迫信号并抑制光合过程。
cell.en: Guard-cell closure strengthens while mesophyll cells accumulate stress signals and suppress photosynthetic processes.

tissue.zh: 叶片水势下降，气体交换受限且组织膨压开始减弱。
tissue.en: Leaf water potential declines, gas exchange becomes constrained, and tissue turgor begins weakening.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展明显受限，并可能出现轻度卷曲或萎蔫。
trait.en: Leaf expansion becomes restricted, with possible mild curling or wilting.

text.zh: 未知昼夜时相仍是解释TOC1状态的主要歧义。
text.en: Unknown circadian phase remains the main ambiguity in interpreting TOC1 state.

state.zh: 复水启动水力缓解，但TOC1关联的干旱转录状态可能暂时保留。
state.en: Rewatering initiates hydraulic relief, while TOC1-associated drought transcription may temporarily persist.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞感知供水恢复，叶肉细胞的胁迫程序不会立即复位。
cell.en: Guard cells sense restored water, but mesophyll stress programs do not reset immediately.

tissue.zh: 叶片膨压开始恢复，而气孔与光合恢复可能滞后。
tissue.en: Leaf turgor begins recovering, while stomatal and photosynthetic recovery may lag.

trait.zh: 萎蔫可能趋稳或减轻，但生长仍保持受抑状态。
trait.en: Wilting may stabilize or ease, although growth remains suppressed.

text.zh: 起始时相未知，无法区分复水效应与TOC1周期变化。
text.en: Unknown starting phase prevents separation of rewatering effects from TOC1 cycling.

state.zh: 水分恢复信号增强，但TOC1关联状态可能保留非对称的干旱记忆。
state.en: Water-restoration signals strengthen, while TOC1-associated state may retain asymmetric drought memory.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞部分重开，叶肉细胞逐步恢复氧化还原与碳代谢平衡。
cell.en: Guard cells partially reopen as mesophyll cells gradually restore redox and carbon-metabolic balance.

tissue.zh: 组织含水量较快回升，但气体交换尚未完全协调。
tissue.en: Tissue water content rebounds relatively quickly, but gas exchange remains incompletely coordinated.

trait.zh: 叶片挺度改善，而扩展生长恢复预计较慢。
trait.en: Leaf firmness improves, while expansion growth is expected to recover more slowly.

text.zh: 未指定昼夜时相，使TOC1恢复轨迹仍有方向歧义。
text.en: Unspecified circadian phase leaves the direction of TOC1 recovery ambiguous.

state.zh: TOC1关联输出可能进入复水后的重新同步，而非简单返回干旱前状态。
state.en: TOC1-associated output may enter post-rewatering resynchronization rather than simply returning to its predrought state.

cell.zh: 气孔调节更灵活，叶肉细胞逐步重启光合与生长相关过程。
cell.en: Stomatal regulation becomes more flexible as mesophyll cells restart photosynthetic and growth processes.

tissue.zh: 叶片水力状态接近恢复，但光合能力可能仍有残余抑制。
tissue.en: Leaf hydraulics approach recovery, although photosynthetic capacity may retain residual suppression.

trait.zh: 萎蔫大多缓解，但叶片面积仍低于持续供水轨迹。
trait.en: Wilting largely eases, but leaf area remains below the continuously watered trajectory.

text.zh: 缺少起始昼夜时相，无法判断重新同步的相位方向。
text.en: Missing starting circadian phase prevents assigning the direction of resynchronization.

state.zh: 时钟与代谢可能继续重耦合，但TOC1节律仍可能相移或振幅改变。
state.en: Clock and metabolism may continue recoupling, while TOC1 rhythm may remain phase-shifted or amplitude-altered.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞恢复动态响应，叶肉细胞减少胁迫信号并修复光合功能。
cell.en: Guard cells regain dynamic responses as mesophyll cells reduce stress signaling and repair photosynthetic function.

tissue.zh: 蒸腾与碳同化重新协调，但叶内区域恢复可能不均一。
tissue.en: Transpiration and carbon assimilation recouple, though recovery may remain spatially uneven within the leaf.

trait.zh: 叶片外观接近恢复，生长速率可能仍低于对照。
trait.en: Leaf appearance approaches recovery, while growth rate may remain below control.

text.zh: 初始时相未知，限制了相移与振幅变化的区分。
text.en: Unknown initial phase limits distinction between phase shift and amplitude change.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态可能形成稳定恢复模式，并保留短暂胁迫历史。
state.en: TOC1-associated state may form a stable recovery pattern while retaining short-lived stress history.

cell.zh: 细胞水分与氧化还原稳态接近正常，生长程序逐步恢复。
cell.en: Cellular water and redox homeostasis approach normal as growth programs progressively resume.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合与水力功能接近基线，但节律协调可能仍有偏差。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthetic and hydraulic functions approach baseline, though rhythmic coordination may remain offset.

trait.zh: 叶片维持挺立，但先前生长损失尚不能立即补回。
trait.en: The leaf remains upright, but earlier growth loss cannot be immediately regained.

text.zh: 未记录起始昼夜时相，残余偏差难归因于复水或时钟。
text.en: Unrecorded starting phase obscures whether residual offsets reflect rewatering or clock timing.

state.zh: TOC1关联节律可能进一步稳定，但恢复后的相位关系仍未确定。
state.en: TOC1-associated rhythms may stabilize further, but their post-rewatering phase relationship remains unresolved.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞与叶肉细胞恢复协同，同时保留轻度胁迫后调节。
cell.en: Guard and mesophyll cells recover coordination while retaining mild post-stress regulation.

tissue.zh: 叶片气体交换趋稳，残余光合限制预计继续减弱。
tissue.en: Leaf gas exchange stabilizes as residual photosynthetic limitation is expected to diminish.

trait.zh: 可见表型接近正常，但累计叶片扩展仍可能偏小。
trait.en: Visible phenotype approaches normal, although cumulative leaf expansion may remain smaller.

text.zh: 缺失初始昼夜时相，使恢复后相位关系无法确定。
text.en: Missing initial circadian phase leaves the post-rewatering phase relationship unresolved.

state.zh: 恢复后的TOC1输出可能接近稳态，同时维持与胁迫史相关的调节。
state.en: Recovered TOC1 output may approach steady cycling while retaining regulation linked to stress history.

cell.zh: 细胞代谢大体恢复，但生长与防御资源分配可能尚未完全复位。
cell.en: Cellular metabolism largely recovers, but growth-defense resource allocation may not be fully reset.

tissue.zh: 叶片水分状态稳定，光合与蒸腾接近新的协调水平。
tissue.en: Leaf hydration stabilizes as photosynthesis and transpiration approach a newly coordinated level.

trait.zh: 叶片功能恢复较好，但最终大小可能保留干旱造成的缺口。
trait.en: Leaf function recovers well, but final size may retain a drought-induced deficit.

text.zh: 起始时相未知，无法确认稳态节律是否真正恢复。
text.en: Unknown starting phase prevents confirming whether steady rhythmic timing is restored.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态可能稳定于复水后轨迹，而非完全重现持续供水基线。
state.en: TOC1-associated state may stabilize on a post-rewatering trajectory rather than fully reproduce the continuously watered baseline.

cell.zh: 保卫细胞与叶肉细胞接近稳态，但可能保留轻微时钟重设。
cell.en: Guard and mesophyll cells approach homeostasis but may retain mild clock resetting.

tissue.zh: 叶片功能基本恢复，组织间节律同步可能仍有细微差异。
tissue.en: Leaf function is largely restored, while rhythmic synchrony among tissues may retain subtle differences.

trait.zh: 外观预计正常，但累计生长仍可能低于持续供水对照。
trait.en: Appearance is expected to normalize, but cumulative growth may remain below continuously watered controls.

text.zh: 未指定初始昼夜时相，难以量化残余时钟重设。
text.en: Unspecified initial circadian phase prevents resolving residual clock resetting.

state.zh: TOC1关联输出预计维持恢复后节律，并可能留下有限的胁迫记忆。
state.en: TOC1-associated output is expected to sustain recovered rhythms with potentially limited stress memory.

cell.zh: 细胞稳态大致恢复，残余变化主要涉及时钟与生长协调。
cell.en: Cellular homeostasis is broadly restored, with residual changes mainly involving clock-growth coordination.

tissue.zh: 叶片水力与光合功能趋于稳定，但未必完全等同基线。
tissue.en: Leaf hydraulic and photosynthetic functions stabilize without necessarily matching baseline exactly.

trait.zh: 叶片保持健康外观，但早期生长损失可能持续存在。
trait.en: The leaf maintains a healthy appearance, while early growth loss may persist.

text.zh: 起始昼夜时相未知，仍是长期轨迹解释的主要限制。
text.en: Unknown starting circadian phase remains the main limitation for interpreting the longer trajectory.

readouts.zh: 密集追踪TOC1转录本或启动子报告信号以解析节律变化。
readouts.en: Track TOC1 transcript or promoter-reporter dynamics with dense circadian sampling.

readouts.zh: 同步测量叶片水势、相对含水量、气孔导度与热成像。
readouts.en: Measure leaf water potential, relative water content, stomatal conductance, and thermal imaging at matched times.

readouts.zh: 测量叶绿素荧光、气体交换与叶肉氧化还原报告信号。
readouts.en: Measure chlorophyll fluorescence, gas exchange, and mesophyll redox reporters to resolve functional recovery.

readouts.zh: 量化ABA、脱水、时钟输出和生长标记并记录叶片扩展。
readouts.en: Quantify ABA, dehydration, clock-output, and growth markers alongside leaf expansion.

controls.zh: 设置同光周期、基因型、叶龄和操作的持续供水时间匹配对照。
controls.en: Use time-matched continuously watered plants with identical photoperiod, genotype, leaf age, and handling.

controls.zh: 设置24小时后不复水的持续干旱对照以分离恢复效应。
controls.en: Include droughted plants not rewatered after hour 24 to separate recovery from ongoing drought.

controls.zh: 记录起始授时因子时间，并在不同昼夜时相重复轨迹。
controls.en: Record starting Zeitgeber time and repeat the trajectory across contrasting circadian phases.

title.zh: TOC1标记的叶片冷响应条件轨迹。

geneFunction.zh: TOC1是核心时钟组分，其节律状态可反映相位及冷响应协调。
geneFunction.en: TOC1 is a core clock component whose rhythmic state can contextualize phase-dependent cold and growth coordination.

state.zh: 低温可能开始以相位依赖方式改变TOC1所示的时序或振幅。
state.en: Cold likely begins shifting TOC1-reported oscillator timing or amplitude in a phase-dependent manner.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞启动低温信号，同时保留初始时钟异质性。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells initiate cold signaling while retaining their starting clock heterogeneity.

tissue.zh: 叶片出现早期光合收敛，但尚未建立冷驯化。
tissue.en: The leaf shows early photosynthetic restraint without established acclimation.

trait.zh: 叶片外观预计接近基线，生长变化尚不明显。
trait.en: Leaf appearance likely remains near baseline, with little detectable growth change.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间尚未指定。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time is unspecified.

state.zh: TOC1所示节律偏移可能增强，但表达方向仍取决于相位。
state.en: The TOC1-reported rhythmic deviation may strengthen, although its expression direction remains phase-dependent.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞加强冷响应转录及氧化还原调节。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells strengthen cold-responsive transcription and redox regulation.

tissue.zh: 叶片气孔与光合协调开始偏离温暖基线。
tissue.en: Leaf stomatal and photosynthetic coordination begins diverging from the warm baseline.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展可能轻度减慢，但可见损伤仍不预期。
trait.en: Leaf expansion may slow slightly, while visible injury remains unlikely.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间仍未知。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time remains unknown.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态可能显示首轮冷诱导时钟重排。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state may show first-cycle cold-induced clock reorganization.

cell.zh: 细胞增强渗透保护、抗氧化及膜稳定程序。
cell.en: Cells increase osmoprotective, antioxidant, and membrane-stabilizing programs.

tissue.zh: 叶片开始重分配碳源以支持低温维持。
tissue.en: The leaf begins reallocating carbon toward maintenance under cold.

trait.zh: 叶姿或生长速率可能出现轻微但非特异变化。
trait.en: Leaf posture or growth rate may show subtle, nonspecific changes.

text.zh: 未知初始相位限制了节律方向判断。
text.en: Unknown initial phase limits inference of rhythmic direction.

state.zh: 完整冷周期后，TOC1可能报告持续的相位或振幅调整。
state.en: After a complete cold cycle, TOC1 may report sustained phase or amplitude adjustment.

cell.zh: 细胞冷驯化程序开始与时钟及代谢状态整合。
cell.en: Cellular acclimation programs begin integrating with clock and metabolic state.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合效率可能在较低运行水平趋于稳定。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthetic efficiency may stabilize at a lower operating level.

trait.zh: 叶片生长预计受抑，但组织完整性大体保持。
trait.en: Leaf growth is expected to slow while tissue integrity remains largely preserved.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间未提供。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time was not provided.

state.zh: TOC1关联节律进入下一循环，冷诱导偏移可能再次显现。
state.en: The TOC1-associated rhythm enters another cycle, with cold-induced displacement potentially recurring.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞维持抗氧化防御并调整糖代谢。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells maintain antioxidant defenses and adjust sugar metabolism.

tissue.zh: 叶片在碳获取与冷保护之间形成新的平衡。
tissue.en: The leaf develops a new balance between carbon acquisition and cold protection.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展持续放缓，外观可能仍无明显损伤。
trait.en: Leaf expansion remains slower, while appearance may still lack obvious injury.

text.zh: 未知初始相位妨碍跨循环对齐。
text.en: Unknown initial phase prevents precise alignment across cycles.

state.zh: TOC1所示时钟状态可能形成重复但经低温修饰的节律。
state.en: The TOC1-reported clock state may form a repeating but cold-modified rhythm.

cell.zh: 细胞膜重塑和蛋白稳态支持持续低温适应。
cell.en: Membrane remodeling and proteostasis support continued cellular cold adjustment.

tissue.zh: 叶片维持受限光合，同时降低急性胁迫负担。
tissue.en: The leaf sustains constrained photosynthesis while reducing acute stress burden.

trait.zh: 叶片生长减缓更易测得，但严重萎蔫不预期。
trait.en: Slower leaf growth becomes more measurable, but severe wilting is not expected.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间仍是主要歧义。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time remains the main ambiguity.

state.zh: TOC1关联状态可能反映时钟与冷适应代谢的持续耦合。
state.en: The TOC1-associated state may reflect sustained coupling between the clock and cold-acclimation metabolism.

cell.zh: 细胞氧化还原稳态改善，但节律性资源分配仍改变。
cell.en: Cellular redox homeostasis improves while rhythmic resource allocation remains altered.

tissue.zh: 叶片组织优先维持光保护而非快速扩展。
tissue.en: Leaf tissue prioritizes photoprotection over rapid expansion.

trait.zh: 叶片可能更紧凑，并保持缓慢但连续的生长。
trait.en: Leaves may become more compact while retaining slow, continuous growth.

text.zh: 未知初始相位限制了峰谷定位。
text.en: Unknown initial phase prevents locating rhythmic peaks and troughs.

state.zh: 经过多个周期，TOC1可能报告较稳定的冷适应节律状态。
state.en: Across repeated cycles, TOC1 may report a more stable cold-adjusted rhythmic state.

cell.zh: 细胞维持保护性代谢，急性信号的重要性下降。
cell.en: Cells maintain protective metabolism as acute signaling becomes less prominent.

tissue.zh: 叶片光合与呼吸在低温约束下达到暂时平衡。
tissue.en: Leaf photosynthesis and respiration reach a provisional balance under cold constraints.

trait.zh: 生长持续低于温暖基线，但叶片活力大体保持。
trait.en: Growth remains below the warm baseline, while leaf viability is largely maintained.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间仍未明确。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time remains unresolved.

state.zh: TOC1关联节律可能重复出现，且振幅受持续低温限制。
state.en: The TOC1-associated rhythm may recur with amplitude constrained by sustained cold.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞保持糖稳态、光保护及抗氧化能力。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells maintain sugar homeostasis, photoprotection, and antioxidant capacity.

tissue.zh: 叶片在连续光周期中维持驯化后的功能节律。
tissue.en: The leaf maintains acclimated functional rhythms across continuing photoperiod cycles.

trait.zh: 叶片扩展仍慢，颜色和含水状态预计较稳定。
trait.en: Leaf expansion remains slow, with relatively stable color and hydration.

text.zh: 未知初始相位限制了振幅比较。
text.en: Unknown initial phase limits amplitude comparisons.

state.zh: TOC1所示状态可能维持冷适应后的时钟代谢协调。
state.en: The TOC1-reported state may maintain cold-adjusted clock and metabolic coordination.

cell.zh: 细胞将资源持续偏向维持、修复及节律性代谢。
cell.en: Cells continue directing resources toward maintenance, repair, and rhythmic metabolism.

tissue.zh: 叶片碳同化受限，但组织损伤可能保持较低。
tissue.en: Leaf carbon assimilation remains constrained, while tissue injury may stay limited.

trait.zh: 叶片生长抑制趋于稳定，而非持续急剧恶化。
trait.en: Leaf growth inhibition approaches a plateau rather than worsening rapidly.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间仍缺失。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time is still missing.

state.zh: TOC1关联轨迹可能接近可重复的低温稳态节律。
state.en: The TOC1-associated trajectory may approach a reproducible cold-state rhythm.

cell.zh: 细胞保护反应与基础代谢形成持续平衡。
cell.en: Cellular protection and basal metabolism form a sustained balance.

tissue.zh: 叶片保持光保护和膜稳定，同时限制扩展。
tissue.en: The leaf preserves photoprotection and membrane stability while restricting expansion.

trait.zh: 叶片可能呈稳定的矮化生长而无明显坏死。
trait.en: Leaves may show stable growth restriction without evident necrosis.

text.zh: 未知初始相位仍影响节律解释。
text.en: Unknown initial phase still affects rhythmic interpretation.

state.zh: TOC1可能报告持续低温下稳定但不同于基线的周期状态。
state.en: TOC1 may report a stable periodic state under cold that remains distinct from baseline.

cell.zh: 叶肉细胞维持驯化后的氧化还原和能量稳态。
cell.en: Mesophyll cells maintain acclimated redox and energy homeostasis.

tissue.zh: 叶片形成持续的低温功能状态，而非恢复温暖基线。
tissue.en: The leaf establishes a sustained cold-functional state rather than returning to the warm baseline.

trait.zh: 最终表型预计为生长较慢但结构完整的叶片。
trait.en: The resulting phenotype is expected to be a slower-growing but structurally intact leaf.

text.zh: 初始授时因子时间仍是关键缺口。
text.en: Initial Zeitgeber time remains the key missing condition.

readouts.zh: 跨三个光周期密集测量TOC1转录本、蛋白或报告信号。
readouts.en: Track TOC1 transcript, protein, or reporter signal frequently across all three photoperiod cycles.

readouts.zh: 测量CCA1、LHY、PRR7和GI节律以区分相位与振幅变化。
readouts.en: Measure CCA1, LHY, PRR7, and GI rhythms to distinguish phase shifts from amplitude changes.

readouts.zh: 在同龄叶片测量Fv/Fm、PSII量子效率、非光化学猝灭及气体交换。
readouts.en: Measure Fv/Fm, PSII quantum efficiency, nonphotochemical quenching, and gas exchange in matched leaf classes.

readouts.zh: 检测可溶性糖、脯氨酸、活性氧平衡、膜渗漏及叶片扩展。
readouts.en: Assay soluble sugars, proline, redox balance, membrane leakage, and leaf expansion as acclimation outputs.

controls.zh: 设置相同光周期、叶龄、营养和采样时序的温暖对照。
controls.en: Use warm controls matched for photoperiod, leaf age, nutrition, and sampling schedule.

controls.zh: 各时点使用独立生物学批次以避免破坏性采样影响。
controls.en: Use independent biological cohorts at each sampling point to avoid destructive-harvest artifacts.

controls.zh: 匹配叶位和培养箱位置，并随机摆放及交叉重复培养箱。
controls.en: Match leaf rank and chamber position, with randomized placement and reciprocal chamber replication.
